title: Add 1 by Counting On
description: Add 1 to a number by saying the next number in the count sequence.
condition: Given a number n and asked to add 1 (n + 1) in numeric or story context.
response: State the next counting number after n.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Relies on understanding the count sequence.

title: Add To, Result Unknown
description: Represent and solve a story where a quantity increases and the result is unknown.
condition: Story problem where an initial amount increases by some amount and the final total is unknown.
response: Represent with addition and compute the total.
label: Principle / Model
example: Clare has 7 flowers and gets 1 more: $7 + 1 = 8$.
notes: Kindergarten Add To, Result Unknown structure.

title: Compare Sets as More or Fewer
description: Determine which of two sets has more or fewer objects.
condition: Two groups of objects are shown and student is asked which has more or fewer.
response: Count or match objects and identify the group with greater or lesser quantity.
label: Production / Skill
example: If one group has 6 objects and another has 5, the group with 5 has fewer.
notes: May use one-to-one matching or counting.

title: Compare Single-Digit Numbers
description: Determine which of two given numbers is greater or less.
condition: Two single-digit numbers are presented and student is asked which is more or less.
response: Identify the greater or lesser number using the count sequence.
label: Principle / Model

title: Evaluate Addition Within 20
description: Compute the sum of two whole numbers within 20.
condition: Given an addition equation such as a + b where a, b ≤ 20.
label: Principle / Model

title: Find Number to Make 10
description: Determine the missing addend that combines with a given number to total 10.
condition: Given a number n and asked what number makes 10 with n.
label: Principle / Model

title: Take From, Result Unknown
description: Represent and solve a story where a quantity decreases and the result is unknown.
condition: Story problem where an initial amount decreases by some amount and the final total is unknown.
response: Represent with subtraction and compute the remainder.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Kindergarten Take From, Result Unknown structure.

title: Write Expression from Visual Representation
description: Translate a visual model (objects, fingers, 10-frame) into an addition or subtraction expression.
condition: A picture shows grouped or crossed-out objects representing a quantity relationship.
response: Write a matching addition or subtraction expression.
label: Principle / Model
example: A picture of 5 dots and 3 more dots corresponds to $5 + 3$.
